spec_match.rationale: The inspector demonstrates the autocorrect IDL property, attribute reflection, removeAttribute fallback, and support detection.

interactive.rationale: Users can select different element types, set values through the IDL controls, remove the attribute, and inspect the live batch table.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept matches the feature-index promise of reading and setting element.autocorrect programmatically.

edge_cases.rationale: The page detects missing IDL support and falls back to getAttribute/removeAttribute behavior cleanly.

references.rationale: The references section now includes chromestatus.com/feature/6264645053710336 and the HTML autocorrection spec link.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, readable state labels, and paired button background/color declarations.

accessibility.rationale: Every detected native or ARIA control now has a programmatic accessible name; toggle state is exposed where applicable.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Fresh direct same-origin browser validation found no unnamed controls. Clipboard-only write denials in unfocused automated frames are environment restrictions, not hidden failures.

summary: Removed hidden IDL probe controls from the accessibility tree. Accessibility recheck passed.
